summary: Worked on the Inishie2 stage file src/melee/gr/grinishie2.c, primarily improving .data reconstruction and matching for grInishie2_801FCCDC and grInishie2_801FD9EC. The PR added the grI2_803E4A60 S16Vec3 table, expanded grI2_803E4AB4 StageCallbacks with NULL callback entries, introduced StageData grI2_803E4C00 for /GrI2.dat, refined the stage parameter struct layout, and adjusted flag-bit, parameter-field, and JObj translation accesses in grInishie2_801FD9EC. The decomp.dev report recorded one new full function match, grInishie2_801FCCDC, and improvements to .data and grInishie2_801FD9EC.
what_changed: Added S16Vec3 table grI2_803E4A60 with 14 entries, later referenced by the new StageData object.
Expanded StageCallbacks grI2_803E4AB4 by inserting several NULL callback entries before the grInishie2_801FD4F0/grInishie2_801FD64C callback entry, improving callback/data layout fidelity.
Added StageData grI2_803E4C00 with stage id 0x19, callbacks grI2_803E4AB4, data path "/GrI2.dat", stage lifecycle functions, item/route table grI2_803E4A60, and count 14.
Renamed typedef grInishie2_thing to grInishie2_params and changed grI2_804D6A00 to use grInishie2_params*.
Extended the parameter struct with explicit s16 unk48 and s16 unk4A fields after Vec3 unk30[2].
Changed grInishie2_801FCCDC's OSReport string from a curly apostrophe in "couldn’t" to the ASCII-spaced form "couldn t", which the bot reported helped bring the function to 100%.
In grInishie2_801FD9EC, remapped xC8_flags accesses from b6/b5/b4/b7 to b1/b2/b3/b0.
In grInishie2_801FD9EC, replaced random-threshold reads from grI2_804D6A00->unk30[1].y/z/x with explicit s16 fields unk48 and unk4A.
In grInishie2_801FD9EC, changed grAnime_801C8138 to use xC8_flags.b3 instead of b4.

In grInishie2_801FD9EC, corrected JObj translation sequencing to set/get xD8, add xCC.x with HSD_JObjAddTranslationX, add xCC.y with HSD_JObjAddTranslationY, and add xCC.z with HSD_JObjAddTranslationZ instead of using AddTranslationX for all axes.
smart_moves: Reconstructed the stage's static data near the top of grinishie2.c rather than only chasing function code, producing a large .data improvement from 45.07% to 99.44% according to the bot report.

Used explicit trailing s16 fields unk48 and unk4A instead of treating the values as components of Vec3 unk30[1], which better matched the parameter blob layout and simplified the random-threshold code.
Fixed bitfield indexing in xC8_flags consistently across the state update path, keeping condition tests, assignments, toggles, and animation selection aligned on the same renamed bit positions.
Matched axis-specific JObj calls to their semantic axes, replacing repeated HSD_JObjAddTranslationX calls for y/z with HSD_JObjAddTranslationY and HSD_JObjAddTranslationZ.
Populated StageData grI2_803E4C00 and S16Vec3 route/table data together, allowing stage metadata and callback array shape to line up with the binary data layout.
Made a small string-literal adjustment in OSReport that was enough, together with surrounding work, for grInishie2_801FCCDC to become a new full match.
decomp_lessons: For stage files, matching progress may depend as much on reconstructing StageData, callback arrays, and small vector tables as on individual function bodies.

When a Vec3-looking region is followed by scalar-looking s16 values, test whether the asset/parameter struct should split the tail into explicit scalar fields; this can improve both data layout and generated code.

Bitfield member numbering can be a source of persistent mismatch. If every use in a state machine appears shifted, check whether the relevant flags should be b0/b1/b2/b3 rather than higher-numbered aliases.
String literals, including punctuation and apostrophes, can affect exact matching; avoid typographic characters unless the binary evidence supports them.
JObj translation code should preserve axis-specific function calls. Repeated X-axis helpers for Y/Z values are suspicious and may be a decomp artifact rather than real semantics.
Callback arrays may require NULL placeholder entries to preserve indexing and data offsets before later meaningful callback records.
naming_conventions: Stage globals for this file use the grI2_ address-based prefix, e.g. grI2_803E4A60, grI2_803E4AB4, grI2_803E4C00, and grI2_804D6A00.
Stage functions retain the grInishie2_801Fxxxx address-style names, e.g. grInishie2_801FCCDC and grInishie2_801FD9EC.
The generic typedef name grInishie2_thing was improved to grInishie2_params, indicating the struct is understood as a parameter block rather than an opaque object.
Unknown struct fields remain offset-style names such as unk48 and unk4A when semantics are not yet identified.
Stage data path naming uses the on-disc resource string "/GrI2.dat".
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Add missing .data objects before over-tuning code; in this PR, adding grI2_803E4A60 and grI2_803E4C00 plus callback placeholders produced a near-complete .data match.
Use callback-array padding with explicit NULL entries to align later callback records with expected indices and data offsets.
Represent adjacent scalar halfwords as s16 fields when random/threshold arithmetic uses halfword-like values instead of floating-point Vec3 components.
Try alternate bitfield names when generated code has the right operations but wrong bit positions; a consistent b6/b5/b4/b7 to b1/b2/b3/b0 shift improved grInishie2_801FD9EC.
For matching JObj movement code, check whether the compiler expects HSD_JObjSetTranslate followed by axis-specific AddTranslation calls and a GetTranslation into the base-position vector.
Check literal byte-for-byte string contents in OSReport calls; replacing the curly apostrophe changed the literal to the apparent matched form.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported +236 matched bytes overall, one new match, and two unmatched-item improvements.
Bot-reported new match: main/melee/gr/grinishie2 grInishie2_801FCCDC improved from 94.88% to 100.00%, +13 bytes.
Bot-reported improvements: main/melee/gr/grinishie2 .data improved from 45.07% to 99.44%, +291 bytes; grInishie2_801FD9EC improved from 90.61% to 95.12%, +54 bytes.
follow_up_queries: Search other grInishie or stage files for missing StageData objects and route S16Vec3 tables similar to grI2_803E4A60.
Audit other stage parameter structs for Vec3 arrays followed by scalar s16 fields that may currently be mis-modeled as vector components.
Compare xC8_flags bitfield naming across inishie stage variants to confirm whether b0-b3 are the stable convention for this state machine.
Look for repeated HSD_JObjAddTranslationX calls with y/z arguments in nearby stage code; those may need axis-specific Y/Z helpers.
Check OSReport string literals in unmatched functions for non-ASCII punctuation or spacing discrepancies.
